task_completion.visual_evidence: The Ardour application window is visible with 'Radio_Edit_Project' in the title bar.

task_completion.description: The audio track is renamed to 'Radio Edit'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Ardour editor window, the track header on the left side of the timeline displays the name 'Radio Edit'.

task_completion.description: The audio is cut at the 1:30 mark.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the Ardour timeline, the first audio region ends exactly at the 1:30 (1 minute 30 seconds / 90 seconds) mark on the time ruler.

task_completion.description: The instrumental bridge is removed and the remaining sections are joined without a gap.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second audio region is positioned immediately after the first region at the 1:30 mark, touching it directly with no empty space (gap) between the two regions.

task_completion.description: The final arrangement is exported to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Ardour Export dialog is visible, showing the output file name 'radio_edit_master.wav' and the destination folder path '/home/ga/Audio/delivery/'.
The 'Save' option is clicked in the File menu, or the Ardour window title bar no longer shows an asterisk (*) indicating unsaved changes.

integrity.description: The agent used the Ardour GUI to edit the audio.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Ardour timeline, tools, and menus, rather than terminal commands (like ffmpeg or sox) being used to edit the audio.

integrity.description: The export was performed through Ardour's built-in export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard Ardour 'Export' window is used, not a mocked file manager window or terminal command.

integrity.description: The track was renamed within the Ardour application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text 'Radio Edit' is typed into the actual track name field in the Ardour track header, not in a separate text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The audio track must be renamed exactly to 'Radio Edit'. The cut must remove the section between 1:30 (90 seconds) and 2:45 (165 seconds). The remaining audio regions must be joined at the 1:30 mark with no gap. The final export path must be exactly '/home/ga/Audio/delivery/radio_edit_master.wav'.
